title: vandellia harmandii
intro: Vandellia harmandii, also known as the "snake root," is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Loasaceae. It is native to Vietnam and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating snake bites. The plant's roots contain bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antivenom properties, making it effective in reducing pain, swelling, and inflammation caused by snake bites. Herbal preparations of Vandellia harmandii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
